question: Why did first-pass yield on the brake-caliper line drop 4.2% in Wichita this week, while Kansas City stayed flat?
metric_label: First-pass yield by plant, line, and shift — daily
target_grain: one row per (plant_id, line_id, shift_id, production_date)

upstream_models.description: MES production runs from Rockwell FactoryTalk. Includes plant_id, line_id, shift_id, production_date, parts_produced, parts_scrapped, and defect_mode_code.
Supplier lot receipts from SAP. Includes vendor_id, material_code, lot_received_at, tolerance_spec, and tolerance_actual. Links to production runs via lot_id on the MES job card.
OSIsoft PI process historian parameters per run. Press force, temperature, cycle time, and forging-spec deviation. Sampled every 4 seconds — aggregated to run grain.
Quality inspection defect codes from the MES QA module. Includes defect_mode, dimension_affected, and inspector_id. Links to production runs via run_id.
